title: Chapter 203
content:
Chapter 202

I didn’t know how long we stayed there just like that, even after the biggest orgasm in a man’s history had long passed.

Elara stayed on top of me, breathing hard at first, and then soft, silent breaths as if she were asleep.

I kept her in my arms, one arm around her back, while the other was on her tight ass.

After about fifteen minutes, Elara stirred and got up. She sat up straight, on my waist, my dick long out of her pussy as it had shrunken.

I could have gone another round, but I decided to control my lust for now and check what the system had stirred just now.

Elara got up, naked and all in her glory as her tall figure stood, her expression the same as it usually was.

Her body cleaned on its own, an imitation of the [Cleanse] spell, but I had to say it was better than mine. The circles were too intricate to be called an imitation. Her dress then flew up, just like the first time we had sex, and adjusted itself on her like it had a mind of its own.

Once she was ever so regal, as she usually was, she looked at Valeria and did the same, cleaning her and dressing her up with magic before she turned to me, “I shall join the nobles outside. Wake Valeria and shortly follow me. We are expected to meet the queen soon.”

“Yes, my lady.” Was all I could say at the moment, watching her walk out as her tight ass swayed in her dress, no sign she was engaged in amazing sex just now.

Well, no sign except for that small, just ever so subtle wobble of her legs.

Haha.

I sure had done a number on her.

I turned to Valeria and woke her up. I wanted to wake her up in an erotic way and molest her while I was at it, but I didn’t trust myself to hold back from going another round if I did so, so I restrained myself and just woke her up normally, but even then, I couldn’t help but place my hand on her ass and shake her up from her sleep.

Anyway, she was already dressed and ready thanks to Elara, so she shortly left as well, her expression as stoic as it usually was.

I was the last one to leave, cleaning myself and my uniform before wearing it and joining my small group outside.

Valeria and I stood to a side, along with the rest of the attendants, while Elara stood with some nobles, as only nobles were left in the hall, as everyone else had left.

The lords began to lessen soon as well, as each lord had their turn, and it would soon be our turn.

While that happened, I checked the system as well, which had been blinking for a while now.

[Achievement obtained]

[Blessing merger]

[Grade: A]

[Description: A merger of blessings which occur when two blessing holders are joined at the highest level of ecstasy

The achievement can only be gained once, and the people involved must be of opposite genders]

[Effect: Blessing holders siphon some of the abilities from their partner]

[Blessing partner: Elara von Simon/Carlson]

[Siphoned ability has been completely absorbed]

[The user doesn’t have an active blessing at the moment]

[A part of the user’s bloodline abilities would be put up for exchange]

[Error]

[Two bloodlines found]

[Awaken bloodline: One]

[Siphoning abilities from the awakened bloodline]

[Error]

[Bloodline abilities not compatible with the partner]

[Reaching into the dormant bloodline]

[Bloodline mark found: Rayla ???]

[Activating bloodline]

[Error]

[Bloodline activation failure]

[Error]

[Error]

[Searching for a solution]

[Solution found]

[Activating a part of the bloodline]

[Error]

[A powerful seal has been placed to thwart the activation]

[Bypassing the seal]

[Bypassing of the seal successful]

[A small part of the bloodline ability had been unlocked]

[Bloodline ???]

[Effects:

#1: Affinity with fire +100%

#2: ???

#3: ???

#4: ???

#5: ???

…

…

…

…

…]

[Error]

[Due to only partially unlocking the bloodline ability, a penalty would be applied]

[All related bloodline abilities would be reduced by 90%]

[Fire affinity: 10%]

[Please unlock your bloodline fully to unlock the rest of your abilities and remove the penalties]

[Granting fire affinity to Elara von Simon/Carlson]

[Elara von Simon/Carlson’s affinity with fire has increased by 70%]

[The user has siphoned the blessing of the phoenix from Elara von Simon/Carlson]

[Skill: Regeneration (Passive) has been obtained]

[Skill: Immortality (downgraded/active) has been obtained]

[Regeneration]

[Tier: C]

[Effect: Continuously heals the user at the cost of stamina

The higher the stamina, the faster the effect]

[This skill cannot level up]

[Immortality (Downgraded)]

[Tier: A]

[Effect: When activated, the user is prone to all attacks for fifty seconds and all injuries heal at a 10x rate]

[Cooldown: 100 hours]

[Conditions to level up this skill: ???]

“…”

Eh?

Ehh…?

Ehhhhhh……….?

~~

In this world, there are a few mindboggling types of characters that are born a few centuries apart, probably once in hundreds of centuries, and no one like them follows.

First, there are the ridiculous ones.

Then there are the broken ones.

Then there are total cheat characters. Protagonists and such.

Then there are outright terrifying ones. Such as Elara.

And then there’s Jack.

Now, counting so far, the ridiculous abilities of Jack are making me want to bash my head in the imaginary boundary between dimensions holding two parallel universes apart.

Just counting the miracles he could perform with his dick slash semen was outright broken.

But then, there were more.

The first cheat ability he had was his bloodline. Sure, it was locked, and the only first effect of it was unlocked, and since it was related to sex, I was under the impression that his bloodline was what contributed to his magic semen.

But then again, thinking about it again, it might not have been his bloodline that did that.

After all, the first unlocking of his bloodline wasn’t related to sex but to drinking ten liters of women’s pussy juices. It had nothing to do with a dick. Even without a dick, if I had known about the requirements, I could have unlocked that ability.

Just this much had made him a cheat character. What made him absolutely terrifying, and beyond that, was the information of there being a second bloodline hidden somewhere in him, locked and sealed by someone.

What was it, and why was it locked? I had no idea.

Well, I wouldn’t have an idea if there weren’t a few factors formulating a plausible theory in my mind.

You see, the forced body reconstruction I achieved with a fake dragon’s heart wasn’t something that was supposed to go so smoothly. It was too easy, despite there being many factors that almost failed me.

But the keyword here is ‘almost’.

I didn’t fail. That should have been my first hint, but back then, I didn’t even think of such a possibility.

But now, the very first ability of this hidden bloodline was fire affinity.

No creature ever has affinity with fire at a hundred percent. Except for only one creature in existence.

A dragon.

Sure, a phoenix, a fire demon, or even salamanders, spirits of fire, have fire affinity, but never at a hundred percent.

A hundred percent meant being fire itself.

A dragon was the only existence that was immune to fire, at full compatibility with it, and with almost no drawback when the element of fire was considered.

Add to that that even a fake dragon heart was okay to initiate a forced body reconstruction.

Yeah, Jack’s second bloodline definitely had something to do with a dragon.

And Rayla was mentioned in there briefly.

I remember finding the seal on Rayla and could only wonder how someone could plant something on her that even I couldn’t sense.

It was by a dragon.

But then, it didn’t make sense how I was affected by it. Unless it wasn’t the dragon’s seal that affected me, but my bloodline, my genes passed from one of my parents, and looking at the clues, it was none other than Rayla who was the optimal candidate at the moment.

So I had the bloodline of a dragon, and Rayla was also a dragon, or someone holding the bloodline of a dragon.

It was all too confusing, not to mention rapidly going out of control. I was just a simple guy, living at the edge of this nation, having fun with my mother and sisters, and the nobles of that town, but now, I had two bloodlines, and I was on my way to kill the king.

Yeah, it really was too fast.

And not to mention, that birthed another question, along with many, many more questions, but this one was major.

If my one bloodline was connected to Rayla, what about my other one?

Was it connected to Julian?

And if these two people were the factors for my bloodline, shouldn’t the rest of my siblings have these, too?

“Time to go, Jack.”
